title: MBTA sets $80 round-trip train fare for World Cup games
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MBTA sets $80 round-trip fare for World Cup train service between South Station and Gillette Stadium on match days.
Tickets are available via mTicket app starting April 8 and valid across all Commuter Rail routes on event days.
14 express trains will operate on match days; subway and select bus services will have extended hours until 2 or 4 a.m.

title: New on Netflix and more: Top streaming picks for April 2026
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix releases '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,' featuring Ralph Fiennes and Jack O’Connell, in April 2026.
'The Devil Wears Prada' and 'The Truman Show' are now streaming on HBO Max and Paramount+ respectively.
Upcoming legacyquel boosts interest in 'The Devil Wears Prada' ahead of its May release anniversary.

title: Details emerge on fatal police shooting of 27-year-old woman in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Megan Whiting, 27, was shot and killed by police in Northfield, NH, after reportedly assaulting another with a fire extinguisher.
Officers responded to a 911 domestic disturbance call, encountering Whiting armed with a knife near the injured female resident.
The officer's identity remains undisclosed, and body camera footage will not be released until after the officer's formal interview.

title: Signature Healthcare in Brockton hit by cybersecurity incident
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Signature Healthcare Brockton Hospital is experiencing a cybersecurity incident disrupting information systems and prompting incident response protocols.
The hospital is using alternative documentation and 'downtime' procedures to maintain patient care while investigating the issue.
Ambulances are being diverted, chemotherapy services are canceled, and two retail pharmacies are closed due to the incident.

title: Villa Mexico Cafe is not closing after all
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Villa Mexico Cafe will remain open after securing loans to cover unpaid rent and renew its lease.
Owners Julie and Bessie King announced increased support from customers and elected leaders helped keep the cafe open.
The Financial District eatery plans to extend hours and expand delivery options to improve business.

title: Committee sues former GM over corporate theft of more than $1M: lawsuit
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Pier 50, LLC is suing former general manager Demetri Tsolakis for allegedly misappropriating over $1 million from Committee.
The lawsuit, filed in Suffolk County Superior Court, claims Tsolakis used funds for personal expenses and competing restaurants.
Tsolakis' attorney disputes the allegations, claiming limited financial control and denies unilateral decision-making authority.

title: Roman Anthony calls himself out for ‘bad baseball’ after latest poor throw
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony's poor throws have contributed to the Red Sox's 2-8 start in the 2026 season.
A costly throw from Anthony led to two runs during the Red Sox's 8-6 loss to the Brewers.
Despite solid offensive skills, Anthony's fielding struggles may limit his defensive role for the Red Sox.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras warns Brewers he’ll ‘take one of them out’ if he’s hit by another pitch ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox' Willson Contreras has been hit by Brewers' pitches 24 times in his career, with six by Brandon Woodruff.
Contreras expressed anger after being hit, warning he might retaliate if hit again by a Brewers pitcher in future games.
Following the hit-by-pitch, Contreras made a contentious slide into second base, colliding with Brewers shortstop David Hamilton.

title: Worcester man receives life sentence for home invasion
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Concepcion Rodriguez, 39, from Worcester was sentenced to life for a 2022 Fitchburg home invasion.
Rodriguez was found guilty of nine charges, including armed assault and firearm possession.
The defense claims Rodriguez's habitual criminal status violates his rights and plans to appeal.

title: Derrick White talks beer, being a Bostonian, and a promising Celtics playoff run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Derrick White, Julian Edelman, Zdeno Chara, and Garrett Crochet attended Samuel Adams' launch for new beers in Boston.
White expresses gratitude for Boston's sports culture and sees potential in the Celtics' current playoff position.
Boston Celtics outperformed expectations despite roster changes, with Jaylen Brown and returning Jayson Tatum leading the team.
